title: Why Lewis Hamilton Needs a Specific Type of Car — Explained
description:
Why Lewis Hamilton Needs a Specific Type of Car — Explained

Lewis Hamilton has always been one of Formula 1's most unique drivers.

Throughout his career, he has repeatedly shown that he performs best when a car behaves in a very specific way.

And when that balance is missing, even one of the greatest drivers in history can struggle to extract maximum performance.

But why?

In this video, we break down:

title: Hamilton’s Speed Surge Explained — Ferrari’s Barcelona Upgrade Exposed
description:
Lewis Hamilton's remarkable Ferrari resurgence continues to gather momentum as the Scuderia arrives in Barcelona with its biggest upgrade package of the 2026 Formula 1 season.

After securing multiple podiums and climbing to second in the championship standings, Hamilton heads into the Spanish Grand Prix with growing confidence and a Ferrari team determined to close the gap to Mercedes. title: 2026 F1 Monaco GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: Although Max Verstappen's qualifying brilliance on Sunday came to nought - came to a dead stop on the grid, just after lights-out - there was still a Monaco GP to be run. There were penalties aplenty; there were Safety Cars; there was a standing re-start; and drivers like Ferrari's Charles Leclerc hit the barriers. title: 2026 F1 Monaco GP Friday Practice Analysis.
description:
2026 F1 Monaco GP Friday Practice Analysis.

Ferrari set the pace on Friday in Monaco, topping both FP1 and FP2, but the biggest story may have been the number of drivers flirting with the limits of the circuit.

Monaco is the ultimate test of the Street Circuit Rule: build confidence progressively, understand where the walls are, and leave yourself enough margin to finish the session. The track doesn't reward bravery nearly as much as it punishes mistakes.

title: Machine Learning Metrics Explained | Accuracy, Precision, Recall & F1 Score
description:
How do you know if a machine learning model is actually good?

In this lecture, we break down the most important model evaluation metrics used in machine learning and data mining:

✅ Confusion Matrix
✅ Accuracy and Error Rate
✅ Class Imbalance Problems
✅ Sensitivity (Recall) and Specificity
✅ Precision vs Recall
✅ F1 Score and F-Measure
✅ Real-World Cancer Diagnosis Example

title: 2026 F1 Canadian GP RACE Analysis
description:
2026 F1 Canadian GP RACE Analysis

Canada 2026 delivered absolute heartbreak for George Russell.

Mercedes had the pace, George looked in control, and another huge result was on the table… until the car let go. Mechanical failure ends his race in brutal fashion after what looked like a genuine shot at victory.

But there was another story unfolding before the blow-up: the front tyre battle.

title: 2026 F1 Canadian GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: What a race!  Kimi Antonelli and George Russell fought for the lead until lap 31, when George's Mercedes ground to a halt with a battery issue. Kimi then cruised to his fourth successive victory, leaving the P2 spotlight on Sir Lewis Hamilton (Ferrari) and Max Verstappen (Red Bull-RBPT-Ford); Sir Lewis won that battle. The McLaren-Mercedes?
